etymology.description: 角(角) (jiǎo, "horn") + 刀(刀) (dāo, "knife") + 牛(牛) (niú, "cow") = a knife separates a horn from a cow -> cut up (an ox)
etymology.interpretation: The character 解(解) (jiě, "untie") visually represents the process of using a knife to separate a horn from a cow, symbolizing the action of disassembling or untieing. This etymological composition effectively conveys the concept of separating or freeing an item, resonating with its core meaning 'untie.'

examples.chinese: 他试着解开绳子(他試著解開繩子)。
examples.english: He tried to untie the rope.

examples.chinese: 问题最终被顺利解决(問題最終被順利解決)。
examples.english: The problem was ultimately resolved smoothly.

examples.chinese: 他们努力解读古代文字(他們努力解讀古代文字)。
examples.english: They are making efforts to decipher the ancient script.
